sections: Public owner crates through Direct Substrate imports rows
HCM-0.4 crate ownership and dependency contract through Published Rust API proof plan

repo_truth: Current Handbook workspace remains documentation/control-pack-only for HCM-0.4. The selected seven-document subject defines the HCM-0.4 ownership, operation, DTO, SDK, CLI, Tauri, Substrate-bridge, and publication proof contracts at the current candidate state. Live crate/CLI/Substrate truth and all runtime gates are unchanged; no Rust/runtime work is authorized. This is a fresh read-only review.
allowed_scope: Read and replay every subject_manifest entry against the live repository; review the complete HCM-0.4 diff read-only.

Read the exact authority sections, current Handbook manifests/public surfaces/CLI JSON precedent, prior published-boundary proof scripts/memo, and a discoverable sibling Substrate checkout only to verify stated current truth.
Run read-only schema/example parsing, semantic probes, link/archive/handoff validation, scope/portability, change-detection, and whitespace checks.
non_goals: No edits, commits, new dispatches, global handoff, ledger write, or user task hop by the reviewer.

No Rust/runtime implementation, crate creation/publication, CLI/Tauri/Substrate changes, runtime proof-gate promotion, HCM-0.5 contract/dock freeze, HCM-0.6 shipped-default selection, or new CLI command design.
Do not rely on parent design reasoning or infer success from the verification summary; judge the exact authority and subject.
tasks: Replay the sorted path/SHA-256 subject manifest and independently validate HCM-0.4 dependency and authorization boundaries.

Review the owner matrix and dependency graph for one unambiguous owner per concern, acyclicity, no SDK/transport/Substrate back-edge, credible handbook-compiler retirement, and a non-preemptive handbook-contracts boundary.
Review all 50 ordinary operation IDs for completeness, data-parameterization, semantic-owner/effect correctness, renderer-versus-Projection separation, and explicit HCM-0.5/HCM-0.6 deferral.
Verify candidate and approval persistence plus escalation and memory-promotion request/disposition operations preserve the frozen HCM-0.2/HCM-0.3 lifecycle, lineage, authority, and atomicity rules.

Verify OperationDefinition field ownership, exact version/ref/fingerprint rules, mutability/idempotency/authority consistency, schema graph direction, and capability-discovery honesty are complete and testable.

Verify the immutable bootstrap descriptor removes discovery circularity; profile/schema catalogs are retrievable without inferred defaults; schema/operation/API version domains and deprecation bindings are mechanically exact.

Verify request and response envelopes are field-complete, deterministic, closed, exact-ref bound, safe for sensitive data, and have a total mutually exclusive ok/blocked/refused/error truth table without false success or partial output.

Verify the exact shared DTO, request-fingerprint, extension, mutability, idempotency-key scope/conflict/concurrency/retention/replay, ordering, bound, sensitivity, and nullability contracts are implementation-testable.

Verify write mechanics and authority class are separate and exact for every mutating operation; posture evaluation/persistence is total; semantic-memory, repository, pipeline, and observation writes cannot acquire canonical authority.
Verify OperationDefinition conditional write sets and response write receipts exactly represent memory-promotion applied/refused/stale record sets and prevent authority upgrades.
Verify compare-and-write atomic groups may legally include semantic lineage records and that promotion, posture transition, and memory promotion realize the exact complete record sets and receipts.
Verify governed-record discovery includes artifact promotion, posture transition, and resulting semantic-memory records, with no derived-kernel authority upgrade.
Verify idempotency retained/tombstone same-versus-different precedence and not-applicable/not-established/established response variants are total across every validation stage.
Verify not-established idempotency uses a total validation-stage/terminal-Problem binding for every pre-establishment blocked, refused, and error outcome.

Verify read-only responses exclusively use not-applicable, mutating pre-commit failures exclusively use not-established with exact unique ProblemBinding, and the stage/status/category matrix is total.

Verify unknown/API/definition admission failures use only the closed not-selected variant with exact selection stage and ProblemBinding, while accepted read-only/mutating outcomes use mutually exclusive variants.

Verify preselection uses only the descriptor-bound admission-refusal envelope with null trusted operation fields, closed stage/reason requested-value matrix, exact bootstrap Problem subject/rule, no operation result schema/idempotency/write receipts, and recomputable response fingerprint.

Verify the admission matrix covers malformed/unknown operation, malformed/incompatible API, malformed/missing/mismatched/stale definition, deterministic compound-error precedence, non-null request fingerprint, and no-response pre-JSON boundary.

Verify the crash-recoverable idempotency journal is non-authoritative protocol-control metadata excluded from domain write sets/receipts while preserving atomic commit/replay/abort/tombstone behavior.
Verify memory-promotion disposition establishes before its authorized domain compare so applied/refused/stale each append exactly one terminal disposition and stale never remains pending.
Verify CLI and bridge framing both accept exactly one JSON document plus zero/one LF and reject every other trailing byte.
Verify all shared DTO instance fingerprints have independently recomputable RFC 8785 inputs, self exclusions, non-circular catalog closure, and transplantation rejection.
Verify typed governed-record list/read and applicable-snapshot selection make multi-actor restart workflows complete without repository/prose or process-local discovery.
Verify negotiated API/bootstrap context enters request fingerprints and idempotency scope across compatibility and removal-major boundaries.
Verify the complete Problem category/nullability partition and versioned ArtifactRef tagged locator/no-follow/content-address rules are closed and testable.

Verify catalog pagination is snapshot-bound, complete, ordered, duplicate-free, bounded, expiry-safe, and sufficient for a cold client to retrieve vocabulary, Resolution-stack, and Projection definitions.

Verify same/different-request idempotency races, result retention, non-expiring consumed-key tombstones, per-major bootstrap refs, transport-schema versus legacy record-routing domains, and the complete Problem nullability matrix.

Verify Rust DTO/generated Draft 2020-12 schema parity, compatibility/versioning, closed discriminated unions, discovery, deprecation, and typed SDK rules cannot drift into arbitrary JSON or CLI prose authority.
Verify CLI JSON atomic stdout/stderr/exit/domain-result rules and Tauri same-DTO/no-subprocess/no-frontend-authority rules are complete, mutually consistent, and realistically provable.
Verify the publication plan truly interleaves proof/package/isolation/publication/checksum resolution per DAG node before any dependent is packaged.
Verify the process bridge sensitive-input/leakage contract and per-crate topological package/isolate/publish algorithm close the prior transport and co-evolving-crate proof gaps.

Verify the transitional Substrate process bridge and permanent crates.io Rust boundary are distinct, fail closed, resource bounded, independently provable, and connected to an exact achievable deletion gate.
Verify the publication proof plan distinguishes package isolation, registry-only proof, and current-tip real-seam adoption; prior evidence/current live Substrate claims are not overstated.
Verify cross-document terminology, classifications, proof-gate non-promotion, HCM-0.5/HCM-0.6 boundaries, examples, links, portability, and no-Rust scope are coherent.

Return findings first ordered Critical, Required, Optional, Nit. Each actionable finding must name severity, exact file/line, violated contract/invariant/gate, why it is wrong, smallest valid remediation, and missing verification. Return CLEAN only if no actionable finding remains.

Verify the admission reason-discriminated union is total: every stage/reason has exactly one concrete winning-field evidence shape; missing client operation, API, or definition pins are distinct from malformed values and from runtime registry absence; later fields are omitted; request-object digest ownership is unambiguous; Problem details exactly mirror the winning evidence; and the request/problem fingerprints bind the same exact variant.

Verify API-context admission reads exactly the named request parents/leaves in the fixed order; absent/null/partial/non-object/invalid/client-negotiated/descriptor-mismatched/unsupported cases select one reason and evidence shape; attempted-value digests use the exact RFC 8785 field-path/raw-value wrapper; and Problem details plus code/rule/details-schema bindings injectively bind the same stage/reason/evidence tuple as request fingerprinting.

Verify request_object_digest deletes exactly top-level request_id and the client-supplied request_fingerprint before RFC 8785/SHA-256, binds every other parseable field, and cannot transitively reintroduce either excluded value. Verify request/Problem semantic fingerprints remain stable when only either excluded value changes while response correlation and outer response fingerprint semantics remain exact.

Verify admission request_id has one total valid/absent/null/non-string/overlong/disallowed output and response-fingerprint matrix; client-supplied fingerprint changes are inert; duplicate JSON members at every nesting level are rejected before object construction with no Handbook response; and structured versus byte-oriented entry-point proof obligations are explicit and consistent.

Verify ordinary response request_id is total for valid/absent/null/non-string/overlong/disallowed inputs after definition acceptance; request-validation Problem and read-only versus mutating idempotency variants are exact and correlation-invariant; outer response fingerprints bind only emitted string-or-null; and generic, CLI, Tauri, and bridge pre-commit versus post-commit failure behavior cannot emit a false error or lose established replay receipts.

Verify original_result_fingerprint has one non-circular exact RFC 8785/SHA-256 closure that binds operation/definition/request, semantic outcomes, provenance, schemas, and realized receipts while excluding correlation/idempotency/outer fingerprint; first execution, replay, journal, and tombstone handling are coherent; and every commit-uncertain bridge failure preserves the exact envelope/key/fingerprint and recovers only through same-key established replay without a false response or write.

Verify the bridge recovery-hold protocol is mechanically total: exact open fields and idempotent/conflict behavior, no domain authority or write-set/receipt effect, compaction blocking only for the exact key/request pair, same-key replay across restart/delay/repeated lost delivery, durable established capture or proven non-commit as the only release paths, fail-closed mismatched release, and ordinary tombstone behavior only after exact release. Verify the private descriptor/bundle control frames do not become ordinary operations, SDK domain methods, Tauri commands, or a hidden semantic decision path.

Verify the recovery-hold terminal state is mechanically total: absent/open/released transitions, complete released-tombstone identity and release fingerprint, lost-ack identical replay, mismatched retry and never-opened refusal, non-authoritative and secret-free bounded persistence, namespace-retirement-only cleanup, no result compaction block after release, and no way to skip/reverse the state machine.

Verify the complete recovery-hold state/event table is total and non-contradictory for absent, unresolved, and released state across identical/changed open and valid/changed/unknown release; released open can never acknowledge/reactivate/spawn/block compaction; later execution requires a new durable hold ID; and the adapter fsyncs the complete request/open frame before send and retains it through a durable terminal acknowledgment across every named crash window.

Verify the recovery ordering is mechanically executable without deadlock or premature deletion: fsync the complete private request/open record, send or retry open, execute or recover only the exact request, send or retry release after a valid terminal outcome, fsync the matching released-tombstone acknowledgment, then delete private state. Check crash/restart behavior at every boundary and reject any contradictory sentence elsewhere in the seven-document subject.

Verify both terminal recovery-hold branches persist a closed terminal-outcome variant, complete exact release frame, and all referenced evidence as release_pending before first release transmission; restart must retry only those bytes. Verify crashes before persistence, before send, after ledger transition, after acknowledgment, and before acknowledgment fsync can neither strand a released hold nor reopen, reconstruct from ambient state, or re-execute the request.

Verify `handbook --bridge-control-json` is the only exact non-ordinary process entry point, has closed descriptor-pinned stdin/stdout/exit behavior, reaches only an SDK-private recovery-hold ledger interface, and cannot become a public CLI operation, public SDK method, Tauri command, semantic capability, or hidden decision path.

Verify every private-control fingerprint has one exact RFC 8785/SHA-256 closure and self-exclusion, including key scope, raw-key treatment, open, correlation-aware durable capture plus correlation-independent original result, noncommit proof, release, and acknowledgment; prove nested recomputation rejects transplantation.

Verify pre-state frame admission is total and precedes lookup for size, UTF-8, framing, duplicate members, JSON, unknown fields, schema/ref/pin/fingerprint, and hold-ID validity; the ledger table must handle only schema-valid keyed frames with deterministic unknown/conflict/terminal behavior and no mutation on invalid input.

Verify recovery-control persistence makes no false aggregate bound: records have exact descriptor byte ceilings, terminal memory is monotonic for an active repository-identity namespace, exhaustion fails closed without eviction, namespace retirement is irreversible, and lost-ack replay plus hold-ID non-reuse survive for the whole namespace lifetime.

Verify the open frame contains exact kind/schema bindings, complete non-secret key_scope, and the raw idempotency key only over bounded private stdin; the handler must independently recompute scope/key/open identities, assert duplicate definition bindings agree, persist only the redacted identity, and never leak the raw key.

Verify durable-capture and both noncommit-proof variants plus every release bind the exact open_fingerprint. Child-not-spawned must truthfully represent only never-created ENOENT/permission/pre-creation-resource outcomes with journal_state not_created; any maybe-spawned attempt must resolve through an aborted journal or established replay.

Verify every named private fingerprint closure is exact and non-circular, including attempt and journal reconciliation identities, and cross-record/cross-namespace release transplantation fails even with reused hold/request values.

Verify the full state/event/output table selects one deterministic acknowledgment kind, state, code, attempted-or-recorded fingerprint source, and null placement for every cell; identical lost-ack replay must reproduce the same control_ack_fingerprint.

Verify the accepted ordinary-request and recovery-hold binding is exact, bounded, non-circular, and sufficient for private-open plus later ordinary admission to reject with no state mutation any retained request identity transplanted across operation, definition, expected response schema, negotiated API, bootstrap descriptor, repository identity, raw idempotency key, or semantic payload while preserving sensitive/large-body constraints. Verify atomic active-key uniqueness, same-versus-different-request distinct-hold outcomes, exact-pair association, and compaction blocking across crash recovery.

Verify ordinary idempotency admission has one atomic, total precedence when a bridge recovery hold owns an otherwise-unconsumed scope/key: the exact held request may establish and replay one result, while a different request is refused before mutation with the closed idempotency.recovery_hold_conflict Problem and cannot establish, displace, or strand the hold.

Verify concurrent bridge-open versus direct SDK, CLI JSON, and Tauri fixtures cover exact-request establishment/replay and different-request refusal, and distinguish the typed ordinary refusal from an adapter-local pre-spawn tuple mismatch that invokes no Handbook operation and emits no Handbook response.

Verify established IdempotencyState exposes only the scoped idempotency_key_fingerprint and that the raw idempotency key is absent from every ordinary response, schema/result instance, stdout/Tauri/diagnostic/Problem/manifest/receipt/capture/retained/tombstone/ledger surface while remaining available only in the explicitly bounded private input/recovery material.

Verify control-open admission atomically includes exact domain-key state and active-key state: absent and retained-same are the only states that can create a hold; reserved-same/different, retained-different, and consumed-tombstone-same/different have exact no-mutation acknowledgments; retained-same creation acquires the compaction guard atomically; no refusal/expired ordinary outcome can orphan a new hold.

Verify the bootstrap descriptor pins the generic ordinary-request schema and its exact binding enters request_fingerprint, private open admission, and later ordinary association; top-level schema_id/schema_version derive that binding before selection/ledger access, and cross-ID/version/fingerprint substitution fails without state mutation even when enclosing fingerprints are recomputed.

Verify the private open frame contains exactly the descriptor/operation-schema-bounded raw idempotency key plus bounded bindings, while every redacted ledger, consumed tombstone, released-control tombstone, acknowledgment, response, diagnostic, capture, and receipt surface contains only the scoped idempotency_key_fingerprint and never the raw key.
contracts_and_gates: 04 HCM-0.4 objective/exit gate, Phase 0 non-goals, and Phase 4/6 sequencing
01 invariants 1, 6, 8, 10, 11, 16, 20 and the frozen owner/integration boundaries
05 HCM-0.4 owner, operation, DTO, CLI, Tauri, Substrate, and published-proof contracts
06 PR-001 adoption boundary; open PG-SDK/JSON/TAURI/SUB-CLI/PUBLISH/SUB-RUST gates; BR-SUB-CLI-01; regression rules 49-67
stop_conditions: Return blocked if the subject, exact authority, or required read-only validation capability is unavailable.
Return a Required finding rather than selecting shipped defaults, freezing HCM-0.5 contract/dock operations, implementing runtime work, or widening beyond HCM-0.4.
Do not modify repository state or write orchestration artifacts.
